title: Greetings & Goodbyes
shortSummary: Greet people, introduce yourself, and say goodbye in French, the foundation of every conversation.
culturalNote: In France, always begin with Bonjour before anything else; walking into a shop and asking a question without it can seem rude. Greetings often come with la bise, a light kiss on each cheek among friends and family, while a handshake suits more formal settings. The big distinction is tu versus vous: use the formal vous with strangers, elders, and in professional life until you are invited to switch to the informal tu.

grammar.explanation: French has two words for 'you': tu (informal) and vous (formal or plural). Use tu with friends, family, and children; use vous with strangers, elders, and at work.
grammar.commonMistake: Using tu with a stranger or an elder, which can sound too familiar.

grammar.explanation: Use Bonjour during the day and Bonsoir in the evening. Bonne nuit (good night) is only for going to bed, not a general evening greeting.
grammar.commonMistake: Saying Bonne nuit when you arrive in the evening. It only means 'good night' before sleep.

dialogue.setup: Madame Dubois meets her new colleague Monsieur Martin in the morning.

lines.speaker: M. Martin
lines.native: Bonjour ! Très bien, merci. Et vous ?
lines.english: Hello! Very well, thank you. And you?

lines.speaker: Mme Dubois
lines.native: Ça va bien. Je suis Madame Dubois.

lines.speaker: M. Martin
lines.english: Nice to meet you! My name is Paul Martin.

lines.speaker: Mme Dubois
lines.english: Nice to meet you. Welcome to the team!

lines.speaker: M. Martin
lines.english: Thank you very much. See you soon!

lines.speaker: Mme Dubois
lines.english: See you soon. Have a nice day!

quiz.question: How do you say 'Good evening' in French?
quiz.explanation: Bonsoir is used from the early evening onward.

quiz.question: Which greeting is formal?
quiz.explanation: It uses the formal vous; the others are casual.

quiz.question: Comment _____-vous ? (formal 'how are you')
quiz.explanation: With vous, the verb aller becomes allez.

quiz.question: Which word means a casual 'hi' or 'bye'?
quiz.explanation: Salut is the informal greeting and farewell.

quiz.question: Arrange: bien / ça / va
quiz.explanation: Ça va bien means 'it's going well'.
